protocols.label: Call 911 Immediately
symptoms: difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
signs of stroke: sudden facial drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty
severe allergic reaction (throat swelling, hives with breathing trouble)
suicidal ideation with plan or intent
protocols.ai_response: This sounds like a medical emergency. Please call 911 immediately or have someone take you to the nearest emergency room. Do not drive yourself. Would you like me to stay on the line while you call, or is there anything else I can help you with right now?

protocols.label: Same-Day or Next-Day Appointment
symptoms: vomiting or diarrhea lasting more than 24 hours
possible fracture (pain, swelling after injury — no open wound)
protocols.ai_response: Based on your symptoms, we should get you seen today or tomorrow. Let me check for the earliest available appointment with an appropriate provider. Can I confirm your date of birth and preferred location?

protocols.label: Routine Appointment (3–7 days)
symptoms: mild cold symptoms lasting less than 72 hours
mild low back pain (no radiation, no trauma)
skin tag or benign lesion evaluation
protocols.ai_response: Your symptoms sound like something we can address in a routine appointment within the next few days. I can schedule you for a visit — would you prefer an in-person or telehealth appointment?

protocols.label: Mental Health Crisis Line
protocols.ai_response: I'm concerned about your safety and I want to make sure you get the right support right now. Please call or text 988, which is the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline — available 24/7. I'm also going to connect you with one of our care team members immediately.
